other_observers: Many (like, a few hundred other observers...); Found by Joe Roller, Glenn Walbek, & Cole Wild
description: Small pale gull, the size of nearby Bonaparte's Gulls. Body whitish with tinge of pink. Tail white, distinctly wedge-shaped with central tail feathers much longer than outer. Upperparts pale gray, nearly concolor from mantle to wingtips. Whitish trailing edge to wing on upper & lower surfaces, no black wingtips. Underwing darkest part of bird but still fairly pale gray. Wings long- when walking or swimming gave the bird a very attenuated appearance. Legs orange-pink. Bill short, straight, black. Eye black, looks larger due to black smudging around eye. Gray post-occular spot, elongated vertically.Behaviors: Bird spent much time foraging somewhat tern-like with buoyant flight alternating with short hovers and shallow plunges. Also spent time resting on the water and on a small sand bar. Also foraged in surf zone of sand bar reminiscent of a plover feeding style.Call: not heardPlumage: adult basic
